frames.caption: missed_detection -- log 6aaf5b08 frame 0 (cam ts 315970475049927218). The COCO-YOLOv8n detector output 7 detections (green) but FAILED to see 3 camera-visible GT object(s) (red): pedestrian@35m; pedestrian@48m; pedestrian@36m. Honest: cross-distribution recall (COCO model, not trained on AV2), coarse class map (car/bus/truck -> vehicle), class-agnostic match; a miss = the detector's recall failure, NOT an occupancy claim.

path_blocked_no_box -- log 78683234 frame 66 (LiDAR-only, no camera; BEV render). Occupancy obstacle voxels (gray) block the ego in-path band (blue) at ~4.2 m forward (red star), and NO tracked box (blue rect) within 5 m explains it. This is the H1 expressivity win a box-only language is structurally blind to: either a REAL unboxed obstacle or an occupancy FP -- and the traversal oracle (RELIABLE) says occupancy does NOT hallucinate obstacles on the driven path, so a real unboxed obstacle is the likelier reading. honesty tag: external-fp.
